name: Authlib (OAuth and JOSE Library)

auth.notes: Implements OAuth 2.0/OIDC itself; no external auth required for library use

performance.notes: Cryptographic operations; JWT generation/verification in milliseconds

agent_readiness.notes: Comprehensive OAuth 2.0 / OpenID Connect library for Python. Client and server implementations. JOSE (JSON Object Signing and Encryption): JWT, JWS, JWE, JWK, JWA. OAuth 2.0 client for requests and httpx. Flask/Django/FastAPI integrations. Authorization server implementation. PKCE, device flow, token introspection. OAuthClient for third-party provider integration (GitHub, Google, etc.). More complete than python-jose or PyJWT for full OAuth flows.

gotchas: Large library — use only the components you need
Server implementation requires careful security configuration
Documentation can be hard to navigate for specific use cases
best_when: Building OAuth 2.0 / OIDC authorization servers in Python
OAuth client integration with multiple providers
JWT/JWS/JWE operations requiring full JOSE spec compliance
FastAPI/Flask apps needing OAuth2 with token introspection
avoid_when: Simple JWT generation only (PyJWT is lighter)
Managed auth (use Clerk, Auth0, or Supabase Auth)
Applications not needing full OAuth 2.0 flows
summary: Authlib is Python's most complete OAuth 2.0 / OpenID Connect library. Implements both client and server sides of OAuth 2.0 + OIDC, full JOSE stack (JWT, JWS, JWE, JWK), and integrations for Flask, Django, FastAPI, requests, and httpx. Supports PKCE, device flow, token introspection. Essential for building authorization servers or complex OAuth client integrations. Heavier than PyJWT but far more complete.
